name: The Path of Bacorero (PR-CV 243) — Circular from Aigües
name_original: El Camí de la Bacorero (PR-CV 243) — Circular desde Aigües
region_intro: Hiking around Aigües, a municipality near Alicante, Spain, offers diverse trails through varied landscapes. The region features natural environments such as Ombría del Bacorero, providing pleasant, shaded sections for walks. Hikers can experience panoramic views of the valley and the city of Alicante from various points along the routes. The area provides options suitable for different fitness levels, from easy paths to more challenging treks. Best hiking trails around Aigües The most popular hiking route is The Path of Bacorero (PR-CV 243) — Circular from Aigües, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 57 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route is well-regarded for those with good fitness. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ombría del Bacorero – View of the Valley loop from Aigües, a moderate 7.2 miles (11.6 km) path. This trail offers moderate hiking opportunities through natural environments with valley views. Local hikers also love the Torre de Aigües – Preventorio de Aguas de Busot loop from la Goteta, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail leading through varied scenery, often completed in about 2 hours 41 minutes. Hiking around Aigües is defined by diverse trails, panoramic viewpoints, and historic landmarks like the Torre de Aigües. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Aigües are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 900 reviews. More than 5000 hikers have used komoot to explore Aigües's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Aigües?
A: Aigües offers a diverse network of over 10 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy walks to more challenging treks, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hiking trails in Aigües?
A: The trails in Aigües offer a range of difficulties. You'll find 2 easy routes, 6 moderate routes, and 2 difficult routes. This variety allows hikers to choose paths that best suit their experience and fitness levels.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Aigües?
A: The hiking trails in Aigües are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 900 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse scenery, panoramic views, and the well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or historical sites to see while hiking in Aigües?
A: Yes, Aigües features historical landmarks along its trails. The &lt;strong&gt;Torre de Aigües&lt;/strong&gt;, a restored 14th-century defense tower, is a significant point of interest. You can explore it on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10323276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Aigües – Preventorio de Aguas de Busot loop from la Goteta&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2838223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Busot&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5703151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villajoyosa Old Town&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find trails with panoramic views in Aigües?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Aigües offer stunning panoramic views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31415695" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alicante – Torre de Aigües loop from Aigües&lt;/a&gt; provides scenic vistas of the valley and the city of Alicante. The region's elevated points frequently offer expansive outlooks over the surrounding natural landscapes.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or unique natural features to discover near Aigües?
A: Yes, the area around Aigües has some interesting natural features. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6349027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall on the Amadorio River&lt;/a&gt; and unique geological formations known as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5456747" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fairy Chimneys&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6324366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de Aigües Baixes&lt;/a&gt; is another notable gorge in the vicinity.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Aigües?
A: Yes, Aigües is well-suited for circular hikes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868629783/the-path-of-bacorero-pr-cv-243-circular-from-aiguees" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Path of Bacorero (PR-CV 243)&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate circular route starting from Aigües. Another excellent loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14633908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ombría del Bacorero – View of the Valley loop from Aigües&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners or families in Aigües?
A: Aigües offers 2 easy routes that are great for beginners or families looking for a less strenuous walk. These paths provide a gentle introduction to the region's natural beauty without requiring extensive hiking experience.

Q: Is Aigües a good destination for dog-friendly hiking?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on all trails can vary, many natural areas in Spain are generally welcoming to well-behaved dogs on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage at trailheads for any specific restrictions in Aigües, especially concerning nature reserves or protected areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Aigües?
A: The best time to hike in Aigües is typically during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). Summers can be very warm, so if hiking during this period, it's advisable to start early in the morning, carry plenty of water, and choose shaded routes like those in Ombría del Bacorero.

Q: Are there shaded sections on the trails in Aigües?
A: Yes, the region includes natural environments like &lt;strong&gt;Ombría del Bacorero&lt;/strong&gt;, which means 'shade of Bacorero'. These areas provide pleasant, shaded sections ideal for walks, especially welcome during warmer days.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Aigües?
A: Many hiking routes in Aigües, particularly those starting directly from the municipality, offer convenient parking options. Specific trailhead parking information is often available on individual route descriptions. It's generally advisable to look for designated parking areas within or on the outskirts of Aigües before starting your hike.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking trails in Aigües?
A: Aigües, as a municipality, offers local amenities including cafes and restaurants where you can refresh after a hike. For accommodation, you'll find options within Aigües itself or in nearby towns, providing convenient bases for exploring the hiking trails.
tips: This site offers amazing panoramic views. It is a good place to go both at sunrise and sunset, although throughout the day you will enjoy pleasant views. It is recommended to bring water for the journey as there are no recharging sources.

Also known for being an old spa and casino in the area, the ruins undoubtedly have their charm and mystery and are a must stop when taking the path to Ombría del Bacorero. The views from here are also very nice.
